species: Here, we report the use of cryoelectron microscopy (cryo-EM) to define the composition of a raw bovine retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) lysate.
tissue: Here, we report the use of cryoelectron microscopy (cryo-EM) to define the composition of a raw bovine retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) lysate.
cell_type: Retinal pigmented epithelium (RPE) cells were isolated by dissecting individual bovine eyes.
disease_state: Interestingly, dysfunction of these important proteins has been directly linked to several neurodegenerative disorders, including Huntington’s disease...
sample_source: Fresh bovine eyes (calf or adult) from both male and female animals were obtained from a local slaughterhouse.
